Question: Distances along a direction perpendicular to the relative motion of the two frames are the same in both frames. Why then are velocities perpendicular to the x -direction different in the two frames?
Answer: Although displacements perpendicular to the relative motion are the same in both frames of reference, the time interval between events differ, and differences in dt and $$d t \prime$$ lead to different velocities seen from the two frames.
